Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

what about soul size?

Philosophy · asked by user [] · 2008-02-02 · 5 answers
soul is spiritual, then why there is a mention of its size in Bhagavad Gita?
user [38] · 2008-02-03
Welcome, Prabhu.

soul size: This is to give an idea how small it is comparing to our material experience.

Btw, you can find answers to your questions by searching thru this site and as well as at VEDA site in my profile.
user [192] · 2008-02-05
...the soul is described as being 1/ 10,000 th the size of the tip of a human hair,spiritual things also have dimensions,the are not formeless or invisible always.
user [2] · 2008-02-05
soul is antimatter, as expressed in "Easy Journey". Cannot be measured by material means.
user [192] · 2008-02-05
Srila Prabhupada , Bhagavad-gita 4.12 , Vrndavana, August 4, 1974 :

"In the previous verse we have discussed, ye yatha mam prapadyante tams tathaiva bhajamy aham. Krsna is the Supreme Father. He'92s very loving father. So He has given freedom to His sons. We are, all sons. Mamaivamso jiva-bhutah. Aham bija-pradah pita. Krsna says, '93I am the bija-pradah pita. Material nature is the mother.'94 Tasam yonir mahad brahma aham bija-pradah pita. So the original father is Krsna, and mother is this material nature. So I am the soul, spirit soul, very minute position. My, my magnitude is very, very small. That is also given in the sastra: kesagra-sata-bhagasya satadha kalpitasya. One ten-thousandth part of the tip of the hair. That is my magnitude."
user [207] · 2008-02-05
Can we see consciousness? If we see this gross material body without subtle nature, then we are on (no better than animal societies) platform of life, of eating, sleeping, mating, and some defense. I agree with Mishra, the description of molecular size is given but can not be measured -- just perceived imho.
